channel.title: cliffy_jr TikTok
channel.description: OK Boomer, all the latest TikToks from cliffy_jr

item.title: Be Careful of Big Cracks in the Ground Road imperfections like deep cracks, tar snakes, or longitudinal grooves can catch your tires and "track" your bike in a direction you didn't intend. If you encounter these, stay relaxed and avoid over-correcting. Ke
item.description: Be Careful of Big Cracks in the Ground Road imperfections like deep cracks, tar snakes, or longitudinal grooves can catch your tires and "track" your bike in a direction you didn't intend. If you encounter these, stay relaxed and avoid over-correcting. Ke
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Drop a Gear Sometimes the best way to handle a situation is to simply drop a gear. Downshifting puts you back into the "power band" of your engine, giving you the immediate throttle response needed to pass a slow vehicle or escape a potential hazard. It’s
item.description: Drop a Gear Sometimes the best way to handle a situation is to simply drop a gear. Downshifting puts you back into the "power band" of your engine, giving you the immediate throttle response needed to pass a slow vehicle or escape a potential hazard. It’s
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Why 650s are the Best Sport Bikes to Start On The 650cc class strikes the perfect balance for a new rider. Unlike a 600cc supersport, which has a twitchy, high-strung power delivery, a 650cc parallel-twin offers predictable torque and a more comfortable,
item.description: Why 650s are the Best Sport Bikes to Start On The 650cc class strikes the perfect balance for a new rider. Unlike a 600cc supersport, which has a twitchy, high-strung power delivery, a 650cc parallel-twin offers predictable torque and a more comfortable,
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Best Beginner Cruiser: The Honda Rebel The Honda Rebel is a staple for new riders for a reason. Its low seat height allows almost anyone to put both feet flat on the ground, which is a massive confidence booster when learning to balance. Combined with a l
item.description: Best Beginner Cruiser: The Honda Rebel The Honda Rebel is a staple for new riders for a reason. Its low seat height allows almost anyone to put both feet flat on the ground, which is a massive confidence booster when learning to balance. Combined with a l
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Keep Calm The moment you lose your cool is the moment you lose your focus. Whether it’s a mechanical issue or a close call with a car, keeping a calm mind allows you to make logical, life-saving decisions. Deep breaths and a relaxed grip on the bars help
item.description: Keep Calm The moment you lose your cool is the moment you lose your focus. Whether it’s a mechanical issue or a close call with a car, keeping a calm mind allows you to make logical, life-saving decisions. Deep breaths and a relaxed grip on the bars help
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Expect the Unexpected Riding defensively means assuming that other drivers don't see you. Always scan for "clues" on the road: a car's front tires angling toward your lane, a driver looking at their phone, or a hidden driveway. By expecting the unexpected
item.description: Expect the Unexpected Riding defensively means assuming that other drivers don't see you. Always scan for "clues" on the road: a car's front tires angling toward your lane, a driver looking at their phone, or a hidden driveway. By expecting the unexpected
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Lean Into Your Turns on Freeway On-Ramps On-ramps are essentially long, sweeping curves that require a consistent lean angle. As you build speed to merge, look all the way through the curve to the freeway. Push the handlebar in the direction you want to g
item.description: Lean Into Your Turns on Freeway On-Ramps On-ramps are essentially long, sweeping curves that require a consistent lean angle. As you build speed to merge, look all the way through the curve to the freeway. Push the handlebar in the direction you want to g
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: The Art of Downshifting Downshifting is about more than just slowing down; it’s about preparation. By clicking down as you decelerate, you use engine braking to assist your stops and ensure you are in the optimal gear to accelerate again if traffic opens
item.description: The Art of Downshifting Downshifting is about more than just slowing down; it’s about preparation. By clicking down as you decelerate, you use engine braking to assist your stops and ensure you are in the optimal gear to accelerate again if traffic opens
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: All Bikes are Good Bikes It doesn’t matter if you ride a 150cc scooter, a massive touring bike, or a liter-class sport bike—the wind feels the same. The motorcycling community is built on a shared passion for two wheels, not the badge on the tank. Every b
item.description: All Bikes are Good Bikes It doesn’t matter if you ride a 150cc scooter, a massive touring bike, or a liter-class sport bike—the wind feels the same. The motorcycling community is built on a shared passion for two wheels, not the badge on the tank. Every b
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Mastering the Smooth Shift Shifting gears shouldn't feel like a series of jerks or jolts. The secret to a professional-level gear change is all in the timing and the "hand-off" between your left and right hands. • Slow the Release: After clicking your gea
item.description: Mastering the Smooth Shift Shifting gears shouldn't feel like a series of jerks or jolts. The secret to a professional-level gear change is all in the timing and the "hand-off" between your left and right hands. • Slow the Release: After clicking your gea
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Mastering the Smooth Shift Shifting gears shouldn't feel like a series of jerks or jolts. The secret to a professional-level gear change is all in the timing and the "hand-off" between your left and right hands. • Slow the Release: After clicking your gea
item.description: Mastering the Smooth Shift Shifting gears shouldn't feel like a series of jerks or jolts. The secret to a professional-level gear change is all in the timing and the "hand-off" between your left and right hands. • Slow the Release: After clicking your gea
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: All Bikes are Good Bikes It doesn’t matter if you ride a 150cc scooter, a massive touring bike, or a liter-class sport bike—the wind feels the same. The motorcycling community is built on a shared passion for two wheels, not the badge on the tank. Every b
item.description: All Bikes are Good Bikes It doesn’t matter if you ride a 150cc scooter, a massive touring bike, or a liter-class sport bike—the wind feels the same. The motorcycling community is built on a shared passion for two wheels, not the badge on the tank. Every b
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Smooth is Fast: Eliminating the Jerk If your bike lunges or jerks every time you change gears, it’s usually because the engine speed and the wheel speed aren't in sync. Mastering a slow, smooth clutch release is the easiest way to fix this. • The Hand-Off
item.description: Smooth is Fast: Eliminating the Jerk If your bike lunges or jerks every time you change gears, it’s usually because the engine speed and the wheel speed aren't in sync. Mastering a slow, smooth clutch release is the easiest way to fix this. • The Hand-Off
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: The world of motorcycling is vast, and there is a specific tool for every type of job. Finding the right bike depends entirely on where you want to ride and how you want to feel while doing it. Finding Your Style: Common Motorcycle Categories • Standards
item.description: The world of motorcycling is vast, and there is a specific tool for every type of job. Finding the right bike depends entirely on where you want to ride and how you want to feel while doing it. Finding Your Style: Common Motorcycle Categories • Standards
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: If you’ve ever pulled up next to me at a red light, you’ve probably wondered why I’m aggressively pointing at my gas tank or doing "hand dances" over my handlebars. I’m well aware I look like I’m practicing a solo interpretive dance routine in a helmet! B
item.description: If you’ve ever pulled up next to me at a red light, you’ve probably wondered why I’m aggressively pointing at my gas tank or doing "hand dances" over my handlebars. I’m well aware I look like I’m practicing a solo interpretive dance routine in a helmet! B
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: The Art of Downshifting Downshifting is how you prepare your bike for what’s ahead. It isn't just about slowing down; it's about staying in the optimal gear so you have immediate power if traffic suddenly moves. As you brake, click the gear lever down one
item.description: The Art of Downshifting Downshifting is how you prepare your bike for what’s ahead. It isn't just about slowing down; it's about staying in the optimal gear so you have immediate power if traffic suddenly moves. As you brake, click the gear lever down one
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: What is Rev Matching? When you downshift, the engine speed needs to rise to match the speed of the rear wheel in the lower gear. If you just dump the clutch, the bike will jerk forward and the rear tire might even chirp. Rev matching is the technique of a
item.description: What is Rev Matching? When you downshift, the engine speed needs to rise to match the speed of the rear wheel in the lower gear. If you just dump the clutch, the bike will jerk forward and the rear tire might even chirp. Rev matching is the technique of a
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Practicing the "Blip" Mastering the rev-match requires coordinating a quick "blip" of the throttle. While pulling the clutch in and clicking down a gear, give the throttle a fast, precise twist-and-release with your right hand. Done correctly, the engine
item.description: Practicing the "Blip" Mastering the rev-match requires coordinating a quick "blip" of the throttle. While pulling the clutch in and clicking down a gear, give the throttle a fast, precise twist-and-release with your right hand. Done correctly, the engine
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: City riding means constantly adjusting to traffic, hidden driveways, and pedestrians. When it comes to braking in town, your approach needs to be progressive, not reactive. Squeeze the front brake smoothly to transfer weight to the front tire before apply
item.description: City riding means constantly adjusting to traffic, hidden driveways, and pedestrians. When it comes to braking in town, your approach needs to be progressive, not reactive. Squeeze the front brake smoothly to transfer weight to the front tire before apply
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Lane filtering is a fantastic tool to keep you safe from being rear-ended at a red light, but it requires total focus. Keep your speed controlled—usually no more than 10 mph faster than the stopped traffic. Watch the front tires of the cars around you for
item.description: Lane filtering is a fantastic tool to keep you safe from being rear-ended at a red light, but it requires total focus. Keep your speed controlled—usually no more than 10 mph faster than the stopped traffic. Watch the front tires of the cars around you for
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: If your bike lunges forward every time you shift down, your engine and wheel speeds are fighting each other. Smooth downshifting is all about a controlled clutch release. As you decelerate and click down a gear, ease the clutch out progressively to allow
item.description: If your bike lunges forward every time you shift down, your engine and wheel speeds are fighting each other. Smooth downshifting is all about a controlled clutch release. As you decelerate and click down a gear, ease the clutch out progressively to allow
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Coming to a halt at a red light is more than just pulling the brakes. As you slow down, click through your gears down to first so you are always ready to move in an emergency. Keep an eye on your mirrors until the car behind you has come to a complete sto
item.description: Coming to a halt at a red light is more than just pulling the brakes. As you slow down, click through your gears down to first so you are always ready to move in an emergency. Keep an eye on your mirrors until the car behind you has come to a complete sto
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Lane filtering is a fantastic tool to keep you safe from being rear-ended at a red light, but it requires total focus. Keep your speed controlled—usually no more than 10 mph faster than the stopped traffic. Watch the front tires of the cars around you for
item.description: Lane filtering is a fantastic tool to keep you safe from being rear-ended at a red light, but it requires total focus. Keep your speed controlled—usually no more than 10 mph faster than the stopped traffic. Watch the front tires of the cars around you for
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Left turns can be nerve-wracking because you're crossing oncoming traffic, but building confidence is all about your setup. Slow down and downshift before you start turning so your chassis stays stable. Keep your head up, turn your chin, and look exactly
item.description: Left turns can be nerve-wracking because you're crossing oncoming traffic, but building confidence is all about your setup. Slow down and downshift before you start turning so your chassis stays stable. Keep your head up, turn your chin, and look exactly
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Staying safe on two wheels isn't about luck; it's about control. The three absolute keys to longevity as a rider are proper training, quality protective gear, and consistent bike maintenance. Formal training builds your muscle memory, great gear protects
item.description: Staying safe on two wheels isn't about luck; it's about control. The three absolute keys to longevity as a rider are proper training, quality protective gear, and consistent bike maintenance. Formal training builds your muscle memory, great gear protects
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: A common mistake new riders make is spending their entire budget on a motorcycle, leaving nothing for proper protection. Always buy high-quality riding gear first, then shop for the bike. Your helmet, jacket, gloves, and boots are your only line of defens
item.description: A common mistake new riders make is spending their entire budget on a motorcycle, leaving nothing for proper protection. Always buy high-quality riding gear first, then shop for the bike. Your helmet, jacket, gloves, and boots are your only line of defens
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: If you look closely, the rave scene and the motorcycle community are actually cut from the same cloth. Both groups are built on a foundation of respect, looking out for one another, and just wanting to enjoy life to the fullest. Whether it’s sharing a wav
item.description: If you look closely, the rave scene and the motorcycle community are actually cut from the same cloth. Both groups are built on a foundation of respect, looking out for one another, and just wanting to enjoy life to the fullest. Whether it’s sharing a wav
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: A tight U-turn can be intimidating, but the secret lies entirely in your eyes and your clutch hand. Don’t look at the curb you’re trying to avoid; turn your head completely and look over your shoulder to exactly where you want the bike to go. Keep the eng
item.description: A tight U-turn can be intimidating, but the secret lies entirely in your eyes and your clutch hand. Don’t look at the curb you’re trying to avoid; turn your head completely and look over your shoulder to exactly where you want the bike to go. Keep the eng
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Pulling up to a gas pump on a motorcycle requires a little more strategy than doing it in a car. Always stay on the bike while fueling to ensure you have full control over the nozzle, and never leave the ignition on. Watch out for any accidental splashes
item.description: Pulling up to a gas pump on a motorcycle requires a little more strategy than doing it in a car. Always stay on the bike while fueling to ensure you have full control over the nozzle, and never leave the ignition on. Watch out for any accidental splashes
item.author: cliffy_jr

item.title: Did you know that how your bike sits at the pump changes how much fuel you can actually take? If you leave your motorcycle resting on its sidestand, the lean angle creates an air pocket, meaning you'll hit the filler neck before the tank is truly full. Ca
item.description: Did you know that how your bike sits at the pump changes how much fuel you can actually take? If you leave your motorcycle resting on its sidestand, the lean angle creates an air pocket, meaning you'll hit the filler neck before the tank is truly full. Ca
item.author: cliffy_jr
